note: Μinuscule "rectangulaire aplati ou écrasé" | Text written in an upright rectangular calligraphic minuscule with thick and pronounced strokes hanging from the ruled line. A notable peculiarity is the letter ζ "a proboscide", in the form of a three inclined to the left. This mode of writing is reminiscent of Salentine manuscripts written in "style rectangulaire aplati ou écrasé"
Each side of the bifolio preserves 22–23 lines of text, with 7 mm between and arranged in a single column
The upper margin measures 35 mm, the outer 45 mm, and the lower 35 mm (f. 85v). The original manuscript measured approximately 190x130 mm
Prickings are still visible in the outer margin of f. 80, their shape elongated rather than round, and likely made with a penknife. The ruled lines correspond to Sautel-Leroy type 24D1

cataloguer.message: Description and identification of undertexts as part of SPP (2011-2016). Migrated to SMDL in 2025.

program.label: Sinai Palimpsests Project
program.description: Imaged and studied as part of the Sinai Palimpsests Project (2011–2016), a collaboration between St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, the Early Manuscripts Electronic Library, and UCLA Library, funded by the Arcadia Fund and the Ahmanson Foundation. The project used multispectral imaging and scholarly analysis to recover and document erased and obscured texts in palimpsest manuscripts from the monastery’s collection.
